reasoning: Tom Brady retired from playing in the NFL following the 2022 season (in early 2023). On October 15, 2024, NFL team owners unanimously approved his bid to purchase a 5% minority stake in the Las Vegas Raiders. According to the NFL's Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) and league rules, active players are strictly prohibited from holding ownership stakes in an NFL franchise. To bypass this, it would require a nearly impossible vote of approval from other team owners or for him to completely divest his newly acquired slice of ownership. In addition to his ownership duties, Brady is now firmly established in his highly lucrative 10-year, $375 million broadcasting role as the lead color commentator for Fox, which he has confirmed he will return to for the 2025 season. At age 47 (turning 48 in August 2025), a return to the field is both physically extraordinary and contractually/legally prohibited by his current ownership status. The market traded at 4% earlier and there is zero recent credible traction for him playing again. The April 2024 comments in the prompt happened before his ownership was officially approved in October 2024. Therefore, I assign a 2% probability.
